events.title: H2 FY26 MPS — Repo held at 10%, SDF cut 50bps to 7.50%
events.summary: Bangladesh Bank kept the policy rate unchanged at 10% amid inflation hovering above target. SDF rate cut 50bps to 7.50% to discourage banks from parking excess liquidity at the central bank. SLF maintained at 11.50%. Daily CRR minimum reduced from 3.5% to 3.0%. Governor stated rates won't be cut until inflation falls below 7%.

events.title: SDF rate cut 50bps to 7.50%
events.summary: Standing Deposit Facility rate reduced to discourage banks from parking excess liquidity at BB and instead channel funds to the private sector.

events.title: Repo rate hiked 50bps to 10.00%
events.summary: Bangladesh Bank raised the repo rate to 10% — the highest level in over a decade — to combat persistent double-digit inflation. IMF-supported program conditionality also factored into the decision.

events.title: January 2025 MPS — Rates held, contractionary stance maintained
events.summary: BB held all policy rates steady in the January MPS, citing persistent inflationary pressures. Signaled readiness to ease only if inflation falls sustainably below 7%.

events.title: Repo rate hiked 100bps to 9.50%
events.summary: Aggressive 100bps hike to combat double-digit inflation. Brought the repo rate to its highest level since 2012 at the time.

events.title: CRR maintained at 4.00%
events.summary: BB kept CRR unchanged despite tightening the policy rate, choosing to manage liquidity primarily through the rate corridor rather than reserve requirements.
